input.background: The ANOVA in Exercises 13.3 found that birth weight of four groups of infants differed significantly according to the smoking status of mothers (P=0.042). Table 13.3 lists the data for the analysis. Data are also available online in the file SMOK_BW.SAV.
input.question: Calculate 95% confidence intervals for all possible mean differences incorporating a Bonferroni adjustment.

smoking_status.description: Smoking status of mothers categorized into four groups.

birth_weight.value: Values for birth weights of infants corresponding to each smoking status group (data not explicitly provided in the prompt).
birth_weight.description: Birth weights of infants, measured in grams, corresponding to the smoking status of their mothers.

mean_difference.value: Mean differences between birth weights of infants across all pairs of smoking status groups.
mean_difference.description: Calculated mean differences in birth weights between groups based on smoking status.

confidence_interval.value: 95% confidence intervals for the mean differences between groups, incorporating Bonferroni adjustment.
confidence_interval.description: Confidence intervals that quantify the uncertainty in the mean differences between smoking status groups.

meta_info.caption: Smoking and birth weight, post hoc comparisons.
